steps.title: Identify the key element the quotation must illustrate
steps.content: The claim's central point is that Richard is “not entirely ready to show subservience” — this is what the right quotation must demonstrate. The decisive clue is any language about learning or mastering the behaviors required of a subject.

steps.title: Match that requirement to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D shows Richard saying he “hardly yet have learnʼd / To insinuate, flatter, bow, and bend my knee,” which directly illustrates his unreadiness; the phrase “hardly yet have learnʼd” captures the exact meaning of “not entirely ready.” His lingering “regal thoughts” reinforce that he's not psychologically prepared for subservience. The other three quotes address Richard's identity loss, emotional independence, or confusion about his status, but none capture that he hasn't learned or isn't ready for the specific subservient behaviors a subject must perform.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
